title: Scale PostgreSQL to HA with CloudNative-PG multi-replica and failover
subtasks: Edit the CloudNative-PG Cluster CR to set instances: 3 (1 primary, 2 replicas).
Configure podAntiAffinity to spread instances across nodes/zones for resilience.
Enable enableSuperuserAccess: false for security.
Set postgresql.pg_hba to restrict replication connections to only trusted pods.
Configure daily automated backups and WAL archiving to S3/R2 for point-in-time recovery.
Verify switchover/failover promotion policy (failoverDelay, switchoverDelay) is set for fast recovery.
Update the sigma1-infra-endpoints ConfigMap with HA-aware PostgreSQL service endpoints (rw for writes, ro for reads if needed).
Apply the updated CR and confirm all replicas are streaming and healthy.

title: Scale Redis/Valkey to HA with replica and sentinel configuration
subtasks: Update the Redis/Valkey operator CR to configure at least 3 nodes (1 primary, 2 replicas) with Redis Sentinel enabled.
Configure Sentinel to monitor the primary and trigger automatic failover.
Set resource requests/limits for production workloads.
Configure podAntiAffinity to distribute Redis/Valkey pods across nodes.
Enable persistence (AOF or RDB) for session durability as required.
Set a maxmemory policy (e.g., allkeys-lru) to prevent OOM and ensure cache eviction.
Update the sigma1-infra-endpoints ConfigMap with the Sentinel-aware Redis endpoint.
Apply and verify all replicas are connected and Sentinel is monitoring.

title: Configure Cloudflare CDN for static assets and SSL termination
subtasks: Configure Cloudflare DNS records for the production domain pointing to the cluster ingress or Cloudflare Tunnel.
Enable Cloudflare SSL/TLS in Full (Strict) mode with an origin certificate installed on the cluster.
Create Cloudflare cache rules for static asset paths (e.g., /static/*, /images/*, /_next/static/*) with appropriate TTLs.
If using Cloudflare R2, configure a custom domain on the R2 bucket and set cache headers.
Enable Brotli/gzip compression at the edge.
Configure security headers (HSTS, X-Frame-Options, CSP) via Cloudflare transform rules.
Document the CDN configuration including cache purge procedures.

title: Configure ingress rules and Cloudflare Tunnel for Morgan and web frontend routing
subtasks: Deploy cloudflared as a Deployment in the cluster with a Tunnel token secret.

Configure the Cloudflare Tunnel with ingress rules mapping app.domain.com to the web frontend, api.domain.com to the API gateway, and morgan.domain.com (or webhook path) to the Morgan chatbot service.
Create corresponding Kubernetes Service resources if not already present.
Configure Kubernetes Ingress resources (or use cloudflared's built-in ingress) with proper path and host-based routing.
Set appropriate timeouts for long-lived connections (e.g., WebSocket for Morgan).
Configure health check endpoints for the tunnel.
Test all routes resolve correctly from external clients.

title: Enforce Kubernetes network policies to restrict inter-service traffic
subtasks: Create a default-deny ingress NetworkPolicy for each application namespace.
Define allow-list NetworkPolicies for each service (e.g., web frontend to API, API to PostgreSQL and Redis/Valkey, Morgan to API and Signal-CLI).
Allow DNS egress (port 53) for all pods to kube-dns.
Allow Cloudflare Tunnel pod to reach backend services as needed.
Block all other inter-pod traffic by default.
Label all pods consistently to support selector-based policies.
Apply policies and verify with kubectl describe networkpolicy and network policy audit tools.

title: Failover testing and ConfigMap endpoint updates for HA services
subtasks: Validate the sigma1-infra-endpoints ConfigMap contains HA-aware endpoints for PostgreSQL (read-write and read-only) and Redis/Valkey (sentinel endpoint).
Perform PostgreSQL failover test: delete the primary pod, verify automatic promotion, verify application connectivity resumes, and no data loss via test write/read.
Perform Redis/Valkey failover test: delete the primary pod, verify Sentinel-triggered promotion, verify cache operations resume.
Test simultaneous failure of one PostgreSQL replica and one Redis replica — verify no service degradation.
Measure and record Recovery Time Objective (RTO) for each failover scenario.
Document all production ingress routes, CDN config, HA topology, and failover procedures in a runbook.
Verify all application services reconnect automatically after failover without restart.

title: Enable TLS certificates via cert-manager for all ingress endpoints
subtasks: Install and configure cert-manager in the cluster if not already present.
Create ClusterIssuer or Issuer for Let's Encrypt (or internal CA if required).
Annotate all Ingress resources with cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer for automatic certificate provisioning.
Verify certificates are issued and renewed automatically for all public endpoints.
Test certificate validity and renewal process.
Document certificate management procedures.

title: Configure OpenTelemetry collector, Jaeger, and SLO alerting for observability
subtasks: Deploy OpenTelemetry Collector as a DaemonSet or Deployment with production sampling rates (e.g., 10% for traces, 100% for errors).
Configure exporters to Jaeger and Prometheus.
Deploy Jaeger with persistent storage (e.g., PVC or external storage) for trace retention.
Update application deployments to export traces and metrics to the collector endpoint.
Define and apply Prometheus alert rules for SLO violations (e.g., error rate, latency, availability).
Test trace collection end-to-end from application to Jaeger UI.
Document observability stack and alerting procedures.
